cards.back: A template definition for a family of functions that works with different types

cards.text:
To declare a function template: {{c1::template <typename T>}}
{{c2::T max(T x, T y);}}

cards.question: What does this function template do?
cards.answer: It defines a template that returns the maximum of two values of the same type T.
cards.explanation: The template can work with any type T that supports the > operator.

cards.front: What keyword can be used instead of typename in template parameter declarations?
cards.back: class (both typename and class mean the same thing in this context)

cards.text: In a template declaration, {{c1::T}} is called a {{c2::template type parameter}} or {{c3::type template parameter}}.

cards.question: What type does the compiler deduce for T in each call?
cards.answer:
max(3, 7): T is int
max(3.5, 7.2): T is double
cards.explanation: The compiler deduces the template type from the argument types through template argument deduction.

cards.back: The process by which the compiler automatically determines template type arguments from the function call arguments.

cards.question: Will this code compile?
cards.explanation: The arguments have different types (int and double), but the template expects both parameters to be the same type T. This causes a template argument deduction failure.

cards.text: You can explicitly specify the template argument using angle brackets: {{c1::max<double>(3, 7.5)}}

cards.concept: Advantages of function templates over function overloading
cards.explanation: Function templates allow you to write one function that works with multiple types instead of writing separate overloads for each type. This reduces code duplication and maintenance burden.
examples: // With template - one definition for all types
T max(T x, T y) { return (x > y) ? x : y; }

cards.front: Can you have both function templates and regular overloaded functions with the same name?
cards.back: Yes. Function templates can coexist with regular overloaded functions, and the compiler will prefer exact matches to regular functions over template instantiations.

cards.text: Best practice: Use {{c1::function templates}} to write generic code that works with a wide variety of types.
